steps.title: Use the memory clue
steps.content: The passage says people gain a lasting place in historical memory by being first. The blank must mean that little can surpass or overcome the power of being the first to do something.

steps.title: Reject mismatched phrases
steps.content: Choice A means vary with, which does not fit the idea of surpassing. Choice B means restrict within. Choice D means go beyond improperly, not outdo the historical importance of being first.

steps.title: Choose the surpassing phrase
steps.content: Prevail over means overcome or triumph over, so the sentence says almost nothing can surpass being first in historical memory. The answer is C.
